title: Sweep stamp fix: honor the state stamp by ITS OWN feature, not the active one; plug the live-state test leak
read_first: .bee/bin/lib/cells.mjs:2140-2156 (the stamp-resolution loop — the buggy condition is `if (state.feature === feature)`)
decision for scribing-integrity D3 (the sweep's source-c spec: default state.last_scribing_run counts when ITS feature field matches the cell's feature)
action: Two defects from si-1, evidence live: (1) SWEEP CONDITION — in globalScribingDebt's stamp resolution (cells.mjs ~2149), replace `if (state.feature === feature)` with a check on the stamp's own attribution: use state.last_scribing_run only when `state.last_scribing_run.feature === feature`. The current code (a) hides a properly-closed feature's stamp the moment a new feature becomes active (live repro: cli-ergonomics stamped 04:02:40 > its caps 03:06/03:23 yet counted orphaned), and (b) would wrongly let an OLDER feature's stamp clear the active feature's cells since the run's feature field is never checked. Red-first test in test_misc.mjs: fixture with a non-active feature whose state.last_scribing_run (feature-matching) postdates its caps -> zero orphans for it; and an active feature whose state.last_scribing_run belongs to a DIFFERENT feature -> its cells still count. (2) TEST ISOLATION LEAK — during si-1's test run the LIVE .bee/state.json feature became 'other-feature' (a fixture name; grep hits in test_cells.mjs, test_cli_state.mjs, test_bee_cli.mjs). Find the test(s) added/changed by si-1 that write state through the live repo root instead of their scratch fixture root (likely a missing root argument or cwd-relative resolution) and fix the isolation; add a guard assertion in the leaking test file's teardown that the live repo state.feature is untouched (read-only compare captured at suite start). Twins byte-identical; scoped red-first; regen: onboard --apply, render_plugin, release_manifest --write, impact_registry --write.

truths: stamp honored by its own feature attribution in both directions (red-first proof for both)
cli-ergonomics no longer counted orphaned in the live sweep
the leaking test writes only inside its fixture; live state.feature untouched after the suite
twins byte-identical; lib_mirror, manifest, ledger parity green

verify: node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_misc.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_cli_state.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check

trace.outcome: Fixed the sweep stamp condition (state.last_scribing_run.feature === feature, not state.feature === feature) and plugged the live-state test isolation risk with a suite-start/suite-end guard assertion in test_cli_state.mjs. cli-ergonomics no longer counted orphaned in the live sweep (verified against status --json).
